content.instruction: Complete the sentence with the correct form.
content.prompt: The classroom ___ (clean) by the students every Friday afternoon.
distractor_rationale: active voice cannot be used here because the subject 'the classroom' is the receiver of the action, not the doer
past simple passive 'was cleaned' contradicts the habitual time marker 'every Friday afternoon', which requires present simple
'are cleaned' uses a plural auxiliary but 'the classroom' is a singular subject, making the agreement wrong

content.instruction: Complete the sentence with the correct form.
content.prompt: These shoes ___ (make) in a large factory in the north of the country.
distractor_rationale: 'is made' uses a singular auxiliary but 'these shoes' is a plural subject, which is a subject-verb agreement error
'were made' is past simple passive and implies the shoes were made in the past and no longer are, but the sentence describes a current, ongoing fact
'make' is active voice and implies the shoes perform the action themselves, which is nonsensical

content.instruction: Complete the sentence with the correct form.
content.prompt: The old bridge ___ (damage) by the storm last Tuesday, so the road is now closed.
distractor_rationale: 'were damaged' uses a plural auxiliary but 'the old bridge' is a singular subject, making the agreement wrong
'was damage' uses the base form of the verb instead of the past participle 'damaged', which is the error of confusing base form with past participle
'is damaged' is present simple passive and cannot refer to a completed event anchored by 'last Tuesday'

content.instruction: Complete the sentence with the correct form.
content.prompt: Fresh vegetables ___ (deliver) to the restaurant kitchen every morning before six o'clock.
distractor_rationale: 'were delivered' is past simple passive and cannot express a regular, repeated action described by 'every morning'
'is delivered' uses a singular auxiliary but 'fresh vegetables' is a plural subject, which is a subject-verb agreement error
'delivered' is active past simple, which does not match the passive structure required when the agent performing the delivery is not the subject

content.instruction: Complete the sentence with the correct form.
content.prompt: The winning design ___ (choose) by a panel of judges at the end of the competition last month.
distractor_rationale: 'was chose' uses the past simple form of the verb instead of the past participle 'chosen', which is the common error of substituting the wrong verb form after 'was'
'were chosen' uses a plural auxiliary but 'the winning design' is a singular subject, making the agreement wrong
'was choosing' is past continuous active voice and implies the subject was performing the action, but the design cannot choose anything

content.instruction: Complete the sentence with the correct form.
content.prompt: All new employees ___ (give) a safety handbook on their first day at the company.
distractor_rationale: 'is given' uses a singular auxiliary but 'all new employees' is a plural subject, making the agreement wrong
'were given' is past simple passive and cannot describe a standing company policy that applies to every new employee at present
'give' is active voice and implies the employees distribute the handbook themselves, which contradicts the meaning of the sentence

content.instruction: Complete the sentence with the correct form.
content.prompt: The suspect ___ (arrest) by two police officers near the train station yesterday evening.
distractor_rationale: 'was arrest' uses the base form instead of the past participle 'arrested', reflecting the common learner error of not applying the correct verb form after 'was'
'were arrested' uses a plural auxiliary but 'the suspect' is a singular subject, making the agreement wrong
'has been arrested' is present perfect passive and cannot be used with the specific past time marker 'yesterday evening'

content.instruction: Complete the sentence with the correct form.
content.prompt: The annual report ___ (write) by the finance team and then ___ (send) to all department heads last week.
content.correct_answer: was written and then was sent
distractors: was wrote and then was sent
was written and then is sent
were written and then were sent
distractor_rationale: 'was wrote' uses the past simple form instead of the past participle 'written', which is the error of confusing the past simple and past participle forms of irregular verbs
'is sent' switches to present simple passive for the second verb, creating a tense inconsistency with 'was written' and contradicting the past time marker 'last week'
'were written' and 'were sent' use plural auxiliaries but 'the annual report' is a singular subject, making both verbs wrong in agreement

content.instruction: Complete the sentence with the correct form.
content.prompt: Applicants who do not meet the basic requirements ___ (not / consider) for the position, regardless of their experience.
distractor_rationale: 'were not considered' is past simple passive and cannot express a fixed, current policy described without any past time marker
'is not considered' uses a singular auxiliary but 'applicants' is a plural subject, making the agreement wrong
'are not considering' is present continuous active voice and implies the applicants are performing the action of considering, which is nonsensical in context

content.instruction: Complete the sentence with the correct form.
content.prompt: The original painting ___ (steal) from the museum during the night, and the alarm system ___ (find) to have been switched off deliberately.
content.correct_answer: was stolen and the alarm system was found
distractors: was stole and the alarm system was found
was stolen and the alarm system is found
were stolen and the alarm system were found
distractor_rationale: 'was stole' uses the past simple form instead of the past participle 'stolen', which is the learner error of substituting the simple past for the past participle with irregular verbs
'is found' switches to present simple passive for the second clause, creating a tense inconsistency with 'was stolen' and the implied past narrative context
'were stolen' and 'were found' use plural auxiliaries but 'the original painting' and 'the alarm system' are both singular subjects, making both verbs wrong in agreement

content.instruction: Complete the sentence with the correct form.
content.prompt: The windows ___ (clean) every Friday morning by the building staff.
distractor_rationale: active voice 'clean' omits the auxiliary 'are' required for present passive and inverts the agent relationship
'were cleaned' is past passive, but 'every Friday morning' signals a regular present routine
'are cleaning' is present continuous active, which makes the windows the doers rather than the receivers of the action

content.instruction: Complete the sentence with the correct form.
content.prompt: The broken chair ___ (fix) by the carpenter yesterday afternoon.
distractor_rationale: 'was fixing' is past continuous active, making the chair the one doing the fixing rather than receiving it
'fixed' is active past simple with no auxiliary, which reverses the agent and receiver roles incorrectly
'were fixed' uses a plural be-form, but 'the broken chair' is a singular subject requiring 'was'

content.instruction: Complete the sentence with the correct form.
content.prompt: All the sandwiches ___ (eat) by the children before the other guests arrived at the party.
distractor_rationale: 'was eaten' uses a singular be-form, but 'all the sandwiches' is a plural subject requiring 'were'
'were ate' uses the past simple form instead of the past participle; the correct past participle of 'eat' is 'eaten'
'have been eaten' is present perfect passive, but the time reference 'before the other guests arrived' places the action in a completed past sequence requiring past simple passive

content.instruction: Complete the sentence with the correct form.
content.prompt: The new safety rules ___ (explain) to all the workers at the start of every shift.
distractor_rationale: 'were explained' is past simple passive, but 'at the start of every shift' signals a habitual present routine
'is explained' uses a singular be-form, but 'the new safety rules' is a plural subject requiring 'are'
'explained' is active past simple with no auxiliary, omitting the passive structure entirely and reversing agent and receiver

content.instruction: Complete the sentence with the correct form.
content.prompt: The annual report ___ (prepare) by the finance team last week, and the results will be shared with all departments tomorrow.
distractor_rationale: 'were prepared' uses a plural be-form, but 'the annual report' is a singular subject requiring 'was'
'was preparing' is past continuous active, which makes the report the agent doing the preparing rather than the object receiving the action
'was wrote' uses the past simple form 'wrote' instead of a past participle, and also substitutes the wrong verb entirely; the correct past participle of 'prepare' is 'prepared'

content.instruction: Choose the correct option to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The classroom ___ every morning before the students arrive.
distractor_rationale: past simple passive refers to a finished past event, not a regular daily routine
'are' is used with plural subjects; 'classroom' is singular and requires 'is'
'is cleaning' is active present continuous, not passive voice

content.instruction: Choose the correct option to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The windows ___ by the workers yesterday afternoon.
distractor_rationale: 'was' is singular but 'windows' is plural and requires 'were'
present passive 'are repaired' conflicts with the time marker 'yesterday afternoon'
the past participle 'repaired' is required after 'were'; the base form 'repair' is incorrect in passive constructions

content.instruction: Choose the correct option to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: Fresh bread ___ at that bakery on the corner every day.
distractor_rationale: 'was sold' is past simple passive, but 'every day' signals a present habitual action
'is sell' uses the base form instead of the required past participle 'sold'
'are sold' is plural passive but 'bread' is an uncountable singular noun requiring 'is'

content.instruction: Choose the correct option to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The injured man ___ to the nearest hospital immediately after the accident.
distractor_rationale: 'were taken' is plural but 'man' is singular and requires 'was'
'was took' uses the past simple form 'took' instead of the past participle 'taken'
'is taken' is present passive but 'after the accident' indicates a completed past event

content.instruction: Choose the correct option to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: These machines ___ by trained technicians twice a year to keep them working safely.
distractor_rationale: 'is checked' is singular but 'machines' is plural and requires 'are'
'were checked' is past passive but 'twice a year' describes a regular present routine
'are checking' is active present continuous, not the passive voice required here

content.instruction: Choose the correct option to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The new school building ___ by a well-known architect in the 1990s.
distractor_rationale: 'is designed' is present passive but 'in the 1990s' clearly locates the event in the past
'was design' uses the base form instead of the required past participle 'designed'
'were designed' is plural but 'building' is singular and requires 'was'

content.instruction: Choose the correct option to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: English ___ as the main language of instruction at this international school.
distractor_rationale: 'was used' is past passive, but the sentence describes a current and ongoing institutional policy
'are used' is plural passive but 'English' is a singular uncountable noun requiring 'is'
'is using' is active present continuous, which requires an animate agent subject, not a language

content.instruction: Choose the correct option to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: After the flood last winter, several roads in the valley ___ for over three weeks before traffic could pass again.
distractor_rationale: 'are closed' is present passive but 'last winter' and 'before traffic could pass again' firmly anchor the event in the past
'was closed' is singular past passive but 'roads' is plural and requires 'were'
'were closing' is active past continuous, which requires an animate agent closing the roads, not a passive state

content.instruction: Choose the correct option to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: All complaints received by the customer service team ___ within two working days, according to the company's policy.
distractor_rationale: 'were handled' is past passive but 'according to the company's policy' signals a current standing procedure, not a past event
'is handled' is singular passive but 'complaints' is plural and requires 'are'
'are handle' uses the base form instead of the required past participle 'handled'

content.instruction: Choose the correct option to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The annual report ___ by the finance director and presented to the board at the meeting last Thursday.
distractor_rationale: 'were prepared' is plural past passive but 'report' is singular and requires 'was'
'was prepare' uses the base form instead of the required past participle 'prepared'
'is prepared' is present passive but 'at the meeting last Thursday' clearly situates the action in the past

content.instruction: Find and correct the grammar mistake in the sentence.
content.prompt: The windows is cleaned every Friday morning by the building staff.
content.error_sentence: The windows is cleaned every Friday morning by the building staff.
content.corrected_sentence: The windows are cleaned every Friday morning by the building staff.

content.instruction: Find and correct the grammar mistake in the sentence.
content.prompt: The letter was write by a student in the evening class last Tuesday.
content.error_sentence: The letter was write by a student in the evening class last Tuesday.
content.corrected_sentence: The letter was written by a student in the evening class last Tuesday.

content.instruction: Find and correct the grammar mistake in the sentence.
content.prompt: The new sports centre were opened by the mayor at a ceremony last month.
content.error_sentence: The new sports centre were opened by the mayor at a ceremony last month.
content.corrected_sentence: The new sports centre was opened by the mayor at a ceremony last month.

content.instruction: Find and correct the grammar mistake in the sentence.
content.prompt: Fresh vegetables are delivered the local farm to the supermarket every morning.
content.correct_answer: are delivered by the local farm
content.error_sentence: Fresh vegetables are delivered the local farm to the supermarket every morning.
content.corrected_sentence: Fresh vegetables are delivered by the local farm to the supermarket every morning.

content.instruction: Find and correct the grammar mistake in the sentence.
content.prompt: All the reports are checked the manager before they are sent to the client.
content.error_sentence: All the reports are checked the manager before they are sent to the client.
content.corrected_sentence: All the reports are checked by the manager before they are sent to the client.

content.instruction: Find and correct the grammar mistake in the sentence.
content.prompt: The old bridge was went down during the storm, so the road was closed for two weeks.
content.error_sentence: The old bridge was went down during the storm, so the road was closed for two weeks.
content.corrected_sentence: The old bridge was taken down during the storm, so the road was closed for two weeks.

content.instruction: Find and correct the grammar mistake in the sentence.
content.prompt: The equipment is store in a locked room at the back of the building when it is not in use.
content.error_sentence: The equipment is store in a locked room at the back of the building when it is not in use.
content.corrected_sentence: The equipment is stored in a locked room at the back of the building when it is not in use.

content.instruction: Find and correct the grammar mistake in the sentence.
content.prompt: The announcement was make by the director just before the staff meeting ended yesterday.
content.error_sentence: The announcement was make by the director just before the staff meeting ended yesterday.
content.corrected_sentence: The announcement was made by the director just before the staff meeting ended yesterday.
